description: Affection or deep emotional attachment.
Intense dislike or ill will.
A state of well-being and contentment.
A feeling of deep distress caused by loss or suffering.
A strong feeling of displeasure or hostility.
An unpleasant emotion caused by threat of danger or harm.
The ability to face danger or difficulty without fear.
A state of tranquility or calm.
A feeling of deep satisfaction from one's own achievements.
A modest view of one's own importance.
Resentment towards someone's possessions or qualities.
Compassion or forgiveness shown towards someone.
A feeling of expectation and desire for something to happen.
Complete loss of hope.
A painful feeling of humiliation or distress.
A strong feeling of wanting something.
Awareness or understanding gained through experience or study.
Lack of knowledge or awareness.
A written or printed work consisting of pages.
A person who is studying at a school or university.
A formal test of knowledge or ability.
A narrative of events, real or imagined.
A written message or a character of the alphabet.
An institution for educating children.
An instrument for writing with ink.
A male sovereign ruler of a kingdom.
Armed conflict between states or groups.
A large body of people united by common descent, history, or language.
A system of rules recognized by a community or country.
The ability or capacity to influence or control.
The governing body of a nation or state.
The state of being free from outside control.
The state of being owned by or bound to another.
A formal process of choosing a representative by voting.
A state in which power is held by the people and their elected representatives.
A transparent, odorless liquid essential for life.
Combustion producing heat, light, and flame.
The planet on which we live; soil or ground.
The expanse of air above the earth.
The natural movement of air.
The star at the center of our solar system.
The natural satellite of the earth.
The period of darkness between sunset and sunrise.
The period of light between sunrise and sunset.
The seed-bearing part of a plant, often colorful and fragrant.
A woody perennial plant with a trunk and branches.
A luminous celestial body visible in the night sky.
Water falling from clouds in droplets.
A large body of salt water partially enclosed by land.
A visible mass of water droplets suspended in the atmosphere.
A large and permanent human settlement.
A large natural elevation of the earth's surface.
A large natural flowing body of water.
A very large expanse of sea.
A piece of land surrounded by water.
A large area covered chiefly with trees and undergrowth.
A small settlement, usually in a rural area.
A line separating two political or geographical areas.
The quality of being fair and reasonable.
Lack of fairness or justice.
A penalty imposed for breaking a law or rule.
A person who sees an event or gives evidence.
An act punishable by law.
Information tending to prove a fact.
A public official who decides cases in a court of law.
The quality of being in accordance with fact or reality.
The state of being untrue or deceptive.
The indefinite continued progress of existence.
The quality of being physically or mentally strong.
The state of lacking physical or mental strength.
The quality of having experience, knowledge, and good judgement.
A combination of qualities that pleases the senses.
The development of events beyond a person's control.
The state of being free; liberty.
A feeling of admiration for someone elicited by their qualities.
A disrespectful or scornful remark or action.
A series of images and sensations occurring during sleep; an aspiration.
An abundance of valuable possessions or money.
The state of being extremely poor.
The physical structure of a person or animal.
A substance used to treat illness or injury.
The cessation of all biological functions.
The condition that distinguishes living organisms from dead matter.
The organ that pumps blood; also the center of emotion.
The organ of sight.
The front part of the head, bearing the features.
The end part of the arm beyond the wrist.
The red liquid circulating in the body.
An illness or medical condition.
The state of being free from illness or injury.
A person with whom one has a bond of mutual affection.
A person who is actively opposed or hostile.
A female parent.
A male parent.
A male sibling.
A female sibling.
A married woman; female spouse.
A married man; male spouse.
A young human being below the age of puberty.
A person who is invited to visit or stay.
An organized group of people living together in a community.
A system of communication used by a community.
A system of faith and worship.
A day or period of celebration.
Sound produced by the vocal cords; expression of thought.
A custom or belief passed through generations.
A marriage ceremony and celebration.
A solemn request for help or expression of thanks addressed to a deity.
Any substance consumed to provide nutritional support.
Fabric or material for making garments.
The place where one lives permanently.
A person who teaches or instructs others.
A person who serves in an army.
A person involved in trade or commerce.
A person who writes poems.
A male sovereign ruler; also listed under Governance.
A person who cultivates land or raises crops.
A qualified practitioner of medicine.
A person who performs duties for others.
A person who sings, especially professionally.
